AtomicTransactionResponse.description:
Single Layer-2 atomic transaction (DWD fact) exposed to the review API.

Mapped from ``AtomicTransaction``; the ``statement_id`` is the owning
``StatementSummary`` id so existing API consumers keep a statement anchor.
AtomicTransactionResponse.title: AtomicTransactionResponse

AuthResponse.description: Schema for auth response - returns user info and JWT token.

BankStatementResponse.description:
Parsed statement envelope with its atomic transactions.

Composed from ``StatementSummary`` (the DWD conform), the linked
``UploadedDocument`` (for ``file_path`` / ``original_filename``), and the
list of ``AtomicTransaction`` rows resolved via ``source_documents``. Use
:func:`compose_statement_response` to build instances.
BankStatementResponse.title: BankStatementResponse

BankTransactionSummary.description:
Summary of a transaction for reconciliation.

Mapped from Layer-2 ``AtomicTransaction`` (EPIC-011 Stage 3). ``statement_id``
is retained as an optional field for backward compatibility with API
consumers; atomic transactions do not carry a per-transaction status.
BankTransactionSummary.title: BankTransactionSummary

BaseCurrencyResponse.description: The effective base reporting currency (persisted override else env default).
BaseCurrencyResponse.title: BaseCurrencyResponse

BaseCurrencyUpdate.description:
Request body to set the effective base reporting currency.

The code is validated against ISO 4217 via ``src.audit.money.Currency`` so an
invalid code is rejected at the request boundary (HTTP 422) and never
persisted; the stored value is the normalized (upper-cased) code.
BaseCurrencyUpdate.title: BaseCurrencyUpdate

BatchApproveResponse.description:
Typed result of ``POST /statements/batch-approve-matches`` (#1001).

Replaces ``response_model=dict``. Failure modes (e.g. unresolved consistency
checks) now surface as proper HTTP error responses (409) carrying the shared
``ErrorResponse`` shape, instead of being smuggled into a 200 body as
``{"success": false}``.
BatchApproveResponse.title: BatchApproveResponse

match_ids.description: Stage-2 reconciliation match identifiers to reject

OpeningBalanceReadinessResponse.description:
Whether the user should be nudged to record opening balances (#949 / AC-ledger.16.1).

``needs_opening_balance`` is true when the user has posted activity but no
opening-balance entry on or before the earliest such activity, so a cumulative
balance sheet would silently omit the starting position.
OpeningBalanceReadinessResponse.title: OpeningBalanceReadinessResponse

OpeningBalanceRequest.description:
Guided opening-balance request (#949): map accounts to year-start balances.

Each account is increased to its balance on its normal side; the net offsets
into the system Opening Balance Equity account so the entry stays balanced.
OpeningBalanceRequest.title: OpeningBalanceRequest

ProtocolFamily.description:
Axis 1 — the wire protocol a provider speaks.

Concrete vendors map onto exactly one family; e.g. Z.AI/GLM and DeepSeek are
``OPENAI_COMPATIBLE`` (custom ``api_base``), Claude is ``ANTHROPIC_COMPATIBLE``,
and Google Gemini (AI Studio / Vertex) is ``GOOGLE_GEMINI`` — its native API
accepts a whole PDF as one ``file`` part (no per-page image rendering) and has
a high output ceiling, which is why it is the provider of choice for extracting
large or scanned statements.
ProtocolFamily.title: ProtocolFamily

RealizedLotResponse.description: Lot-level realized P&L row for a holding detail page.

ReasoningEffort.description:
Per-scene reasoning depth, mapped onto each provider by the client.

``NONE`` means "do not request extended reasoning"; the others map to the
litellm ``reasoning_effort`` parameter (which in turn maps to Anthropic
thinking budgets / OpenAI reasoning levels).
ReasoningEffort.title: ReasoningEffort

info.description: Personal financial management system with double-entry bookkeeping

get.description:
List all accounts with optional filters and pagination.

Set include_balance=true to calculate balances (may be slower with many accounts).
List statement coverage status for active accounts.

get.description:
Readiness nudge (#949): is the balance sheet at risk of being incomplete?

Returns ``needs_opening_balance=True`` when the user has posted activity but no
opening-balance entry on or before its earliest date, so the UI can warn before
they ship a silently-incomplete balance sheet.

post.description:
Guided opening-balance flow (#949): establish year-start account balances.

Posts one balanced journal entry that increases each supplied account to its
opening balance and offsets the net into the system Opening Balance Equity
account, so a cross-year balance sheet is complete from the start.

Surface the held-out replay of the live correction corpus (read-only).

get.description: Return duplicate and transfer-pair candidates for a statement.

post.description:
Resolve a statement's Stage-1 duplicate/transfer-pair candidates (#962).

Records the reviewer's decision so the approval guard stops blocking, instead
of leaving a legitimately-conflicting statement permanently stuck in ``parsed``.

Reviewer specifies the currency for a ``currency_unresolved`` transaction (AC12.40.3).

Validates the chosen code as ISO-4217 (``src.audit.money.Currency``), clears the
unresolved flag, and records the resolution audit (who/when). Only after this
can the transaction be promoted to a journal entry (AC12.40.4).

get.description: List all statements for the current user.

post.description:
Batch approve matches (blocked by unresolved checks).

#1001: unresolved consistency checks now raise a 409 (structured
``ErrorResponse``) instead of returning ``{"success": false}`` in a 200 body.
Batch reject matches.

get.description: List/filter consistency checks.

post.description: Resolve a consistency check.

get.description: List statements pending human review.
Stage 2: Get review queue (matches + checks).

post.description:
Upload a financial statement and enqueue parsing.

Supported file types: PDF, CSV, PNG, JPG. Model is optional for PDF/image uploads;
omitted model uses the OCR-first default pipeline.

Institution is optional for PDF/image uploads (AI auto-detects it from the
document). It is **required** for CSV uploads — the institution cannot be
auto-detected from CSV content, so a missing institution is rejected
synchronously with HTTP 400 rather than accepted and rejected asynchronously
by the parse worker (#1141 / #1087).

post.description: Import portfolio positions from an already parsed brokerage statement.

get.description:
Stream the original uploaded document for a statement, same-origin.

AC16.33.5: the Stage 1 review PDF preview embeds this authenticated,
same-origin endpoint as a ``blob:`` object URL instead of a cross-origin
object-storage URL the CSP cannot frame. Auth is the standard Bearer
dependency; the frontend fetches the bytes via ``apiDownload`` (which
carries the token) and never points an iframe directly at storage.

post.description: Retry parsing with a different model (e.g., stronger model for better accuracy).
